title: Emmanuel, God with us
summary: This sermon explores the profound significance of Jesus Christ being called "Emmanuel," which means "God with us." Initially, God was perceived as a distant entity, but through Jesus, He became intimately present with humanity. This presence challenges the dominance of Satan, who had long been among humans. Jesus, unlike Satan, brought the presence of God directly into human experience, marking a new era where God's power was no longer abstract but embodied in a tangible, approachable form. The sermon emphasizes that Jesus did not consider it beneath His dignity to come in the form of a man and live among humans, demonstrating humility by sharing in all aspects of human life—joy, sorrow, and suffering. This act of God teaches us the value of humility and the importance of not looking down on others. Jesus' willingness to engage with all people, regardless of their status or morality, sets an example for us to follow. Furthermore, the sermon highlights the significance of God coming as a man, making Him approachable by everyone. Jesus, as a man, made God’s presence visible and relatable, contrasting the previous distant and fearsome manifestations of God, such as at Mount Sinai. His first coming was a lesson in humility and intimacy, and it also serves as a promise of His return. Believers are encouraged to live in a way that prepares for Jesus' return, embodying His teachings and values in anticipation of the restoration He will bring. In conclusion, the sermon reassures believers that with God on their side, they can overcome any challenges. The truth of God being with us, both now and in the future, offers profound hope and confidence for living a life that reflects His presence.
